result.markdown:
--- title: "Task 3 Sample analysis" author: "Will Wright" format: html: code-fold: false theme: cosmos filters: - webr --- In this notebook, I run through the climate change analysis for abundance, using the model made in Task 2. # Helper functions ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} get_climate_predictions <- function( wetland, model, spp_code = NULL, fx_group = NULL, flow_metadata = combined_flow_metadata, climate_projections = valley_climate_projections_with_wetlands, basin_flow_stats = basin_flow_mean_sd ) { # --- Pre-processing ------------------------------------------------------- scaling <- combined_flow_metadata |> filter(wetlands == wetland) |> slice(1) |> select( wetland_mean_flow_3m, wetland_sd_flow_3m, wetland_mean_flow_annual, wetland_sd_flow_annual ) # --- Build scenario newdata ----------------------------------------------- nuisance_vars <- c( "asset_precip_3m_scaled", "asset_precip_annual_scaled", "asset_precip_annual_scaled_lagged", "valley_precip_3m_scaled", "valley_precip_annual_scaled", "valley_precip_annual_scaled_lagged", "basin_precip_3m_scaled", "basin_precip_annual_scaled", "basin_precip_annual_scaled_lagged", "swc_australia_scaled", "swc_australia_scaled_lagged" ) scenarios <- valley_climate_projections_with_wetlands |> filter(Wetland == wetland) |> arrange(Scenario) |> select(Scenario, pct_change) |> left_join( basin_flow_mean_sd |> select(Scenario, basin_flow_zscore_3m, basin_flow_zscore_annual), by = "Scenario" ) |> mutate( wetland_flow_3m_scaled = (scaling$wetland_mean_flow_3m * pct_change) / scaling$wetland_sd_flow_3m, wetland_flow_annual_scaled = (scaling$wetland_mean_flow_annual * pct_change) / scaling$wetland_sd_flow_annual, wetland_flow_annual_scaled_lagged = wetland_flow_annual_scaled, total_basin_flow_3m_scaled = basin_flow_zscore_3m, total_basin_flow_annual_scaled = basin_flow_zscore_annual, total_basin_flow_annual_scaled_lagged = basin_flow_zscore_annual, Wetland = wetland ) |> select(-basin_flow_zscore_3m, -basin_flow_zscore_annual) pred_newdata <- bind_rows( tibble( Scenario = "Baseline", pct_change = NA_real_, wetland_flow_3m_scaled = 0, wetland_flow_annual_scaled = 0, wetland_flow_annual_scaled_lagged = 0, total_basin_flow_3m_scaled = 0, total_basin_flow_annual_scaled = 0, total_basin_flow_annual_scaled_lagged = 0, Wetland = wetland ), scenarios ) pred_newdata[nuisance_vars] <- 0 if (!is.null(spp_code)) { pred_newdata$spp_code <- spp_code } if (!is.null(fx_group)) { pred_newdata$fx_group <- fx_group } # --- Posterior draws ------------------------------------------------------ draws <- posterior_epred(model, newdata = pred_newdata, re_formula = NULL) |> t() |> as_tibble() |> mutate(scenario = pred_newdata$Scenario) |> pivot_longer(-scenario, names_to = ".draw", values_to = ".value") # --- Percent change (shared computation) ---------------------------------- draws_with_pct <- draws |> group_by(.draw) |> mutate( baseline_val = .value[scenario == "Baseline"], percent_change = (.value - baseline_val) / baseline_val * 100 ) |> ungroup() summary_results <- draws_with_pct |> group_by(scenario) |> median_hdci(percent_change, .value) |> mutate(wetland_name = wetland) if (!is.null(spp_code)) { summary_results$spp_code <- spp_code } if (!is.null(fx_group)) { summary_results$fx_group <- fx_group } list( summary = summary_results ) } ``` ::: # Libraries and data ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} library(dplyr) library(tidyr) library(ggplot2) library(stringr) library(eawsAnalysis) library(sf) library(brms) library(tidybayes) library(patchwork) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} valley_climate_projections_with_wetlands <- read.csv("data/valley_climate_projections_with_wetlands.csv") |> na.omit() # Scalars for each wetland, based on the valley it is located in for each climate scenario combined_flow_metadata <- read.csv("data/combined_flow_metadata.csv") # This is the mean and sd for every wetland, based on the scaling we did for the models basin_flow_mean_sd <- read.csv("data/basin_flow_mean_sd.csv") # This is the mean and sd for the basin flow, which we use for scaling the basin flow variables in the climate data data(assets) assets_coords <- assets %>% # Ensure we have the center point of each polygon mutate(centroid_lat = st_coordinates(st_centroid(geom))[, 2]) %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% # Sort by latitude descending (North to South) arrange(desc(centroid_lat)) %>% pull(Wetland) %>% str_to_lower() ``` ::: # Walk through for one wetland We get the first wetland, and load in the model ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} wetlands <- valley_climate_projections_with_wetlands |> pull(Wetland) %>% unique() wetland <- wetlands[1] overall_abund <- readr::read_rds("data/m1.1.rds") print(paste0("Wetland: ", wetland)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "Wetland: currawinya lakes" ``` ::: ::: Get the scaling factors for that wetland (mean and sd), because we scaled the input variables on a wetland basis. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} scaling <- combined_flow_metadata |> filter(wetlands == wetland) |> slice(1) |> select( wetland_mean_flow_3m, wetland_sd_flow_3m, wetland_mean_flow_annual, wetland_sd_flow_annual ) glimpse(scaling) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Rows: 1 Columns: 4 $ wetland_mean_flow_3m 16919.5 $ wetland_sd_flow_3m 39504.06 $ wetland_mean_flow_annual 480961.3 $ wetland_sd_flow_annual 576268.3 ``` ::: ::: Scale the predictors by the appropriate climate scalars, and then convert to z-scores using the wetland-specific mean and sd. We also add in the basin flow z-scores, which are used as predictors in the model, and are scaled using the mean and sd from the `basin_flow_mean_sd` dataset. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} scenarios <- valley_climate_projections_with_wetlands |> filter(Wetland == wetland) |> arrange(Scenario) |> select(Scenario, pct_change) |> left_join( basin_flow_mean_sd |> select(Scenario, basin_flow_zscore_3m, basin_flow_zscore_annual), by = "Scenario" ) |> mutate( wetland_flow_3m_scaled = (scaling$wetland_mean_flow_3m * pct_change) / scaling$wetland_sd_flow_3m, wetland_flow_annual_scaled = (scaling$wetland_mean_flow_annual * pct_change) / scaling$wetland_sd_flow_annual, wetland_flow_annual_scaled_lagged = wetland_flow_annual_scaled, total_basin_flow_3m_scaled = basin_flow_zscore_3m, total_basin_flow_annual_scaled = basin_flow_zscore_annual, total_basin_flow_annual_scaled_lagged = basin_flow_zscore_annual, Wetland = wetland ) |> select(-basin_flow_zscore_3m, -basin_flow_zscore_annual) ``` ::: Build the new data for predicting on. We set all the target flow variables for the baseline scenario to 0 (the mean), and then add in the scaled flow variables for the scenarios. We also set all the other variables to 0 (mean). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} nuisance_vars <- c( "asset_precip_3m_scaled", "asset_precip_annual_scaled", "asset_precip_annual_scaled_lagged", "valley_precip_3m_scaled", "valley_precip_annual_scaled", "valley_precip_annual_scaled_lagged", "basin_precip_3m_scaled", "basin_precip_annual_scaled", "basin_precip_annual_scaled_lagged", "swc_australia_scaled", "swc_australia_scaled_lagged" ) pred_newdata <- bind_rows( tibble( Scenario = "Baseline", pct_change = NA_real_, wetland_flow_3m_scaled = 0, wetland_flow_annual_scaled = 0, wetland_flow_annual_scaled_lagged = 0, total_basin_flow_3m_scaled = 0, total_basin_flow_annual_scaled = 0, total_basin_flow_annual_scaled_lagged = 0, Wetland = wetland ), scenarios ) pred_newdata[nuisance_vars] <- 0 glimpse(pred_newdata) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Rows: 7 Columns: 20 $ Scenario "Baseline", "S1", "S2", "S3", "S… $ pct_change NA, 0.15, -0.02, -0.15, 0.22, -0… $ wetland_flow_3m_scaled 0.000000000, 0.064244660, -0.008… $ wetland_flow_annual_scaled 0.00000000, 0.12519204, -0.01669… $ wetland_flow_annual_scaled_lagged 0.00000000, 0.12519204, -0.01669… $ total_basin_flow_3m_scaled 0.00000000, 0.06120610, -0.15229… $ total_basin_flow_annual_scaled 0.0000000, 0.1192174, -0.2921568… $ total_basin_flow_annual_scaled_lagged 0.0000000, 0.1192174, -0.2921568… $ Wetland "currawinya lakes", "currawinya … $ asset_precip_3m_scaled 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0 $ asset_precip_annual_scaled 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0 $ asset_precip_annual_scaled_lagged 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0 $ valley_precip_3m_scaled 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0 $ valley_precip_annual_scaled 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0 $ valley_precip_annual_scaled_lagged 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0 $ basin_precip_3m_scaled 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0 $ basin_precip_annual_scaled 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0 $ basin_precip_annual_scaled_lagged 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0 $ swc_australia_scaled 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0 $ swc_australia_scaled_lagged 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0 ``` ::: ::: Now we predict. We use `posterior_epred()`, not `posterior_predict()` to eliminate noise from residuals, and just focus on the estimate for the mean. We also calculate the estimated percent change from the baseline. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} model <- overall_abund draws <- posterior_epred(model, newdata = pred_newdata, re_formula = NULL) |> t() |> as_tibble() |> mutate(scenario = pred_newdata$Scenario) |> pivot_longer(-scenario, names_to = ".draw", values_to = ".value") draws_with_pct <- draws |> group_by(.draw) |> mutate( baseline_val = .value[scenario == "Baseline"], percent_change = (.value - baseline_val) / baseline_val * 100 ) |> ungroup() summary_results <- draws_with_pct |> group_by(scenario) |> median_hdci(percent_change, .value, na.rm = TRUE) |> mutate(wetland_name = wetland) summary_results ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` # A tibble: 7 × 11 scenario percent_change percent_change.lower percent_change.upper .value 1 Baseline 0 0 0 29438. 2 S1 1.24 -1.59 3.96 29781. 3 S2 0.463 -4.74 6.03 29619. 4 S3 -0.122 -10.7 11.0 29409. 5 S4 1.90 -1.97 5.76 29993. 6 S5 0.692 -7.50 8.70 29690. 7 S6 -0.191 -15.6 16.5 29422. # ℹ 6 more variables: .value.lower , .value.upper , .width , # .point , .interval , wetland_name ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} DT::datatable( summary_results, options = list( pageLength = 7, autoWidth = TRUE, scrollX = TRUE, dom = 'lrtip' # Remove the filter box if you want more compact layout ), class = 'compact stripe' ) |> DT::formatStyle( 0, target= 'row',lineHeight='70%') |> DT::formatRound(columns = c("percent_change", "percent_change.lower", "percent_change.upper", ".value", ".value.lower", ".value.upper"), digits = 2) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Make a plot to show the results (up to you how you want to visualise) ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p1 <- ggplot(summary_results |> filter(scenario != "Baseline") |> mutate(scenario = factor(scenario, levels = c("S1", "S4", "S2", "S5", "S3", "S6"))), aes(x = scenario, y = percent_change)) + geom_point() + geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = percent_change.lower, ymax = percent_change.upper), width = 0.2) + labs( x = "Climate Scenario", y = "Percent Change from Baseline" ) + theme_minimal() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) p2 <- ggplot(summary_results |> mutate(scenario = factor(scenario, levels = c("Baseline", "S1", "S4", "S2", "S5", "S3", "S6"))), aes(x = scenario, y = .value)) + geom_point() + geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = .value.lower, ymax = .value.upper), width = 0.2) + labs( x = "Climate Scenario", y = "Absolute Change" ) + theme_minimal() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) p1+p2 ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](climate_files/figure-html/plot-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: # All wetlands We wrap this all in a function - `get_climate_predictions()` and loop through the wetlands ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} wetlands <- valley_climate_projections_with_wetlands |> pull(Wetland) %>% unique() overall_abund <- readRDS("data/m1.1.rds") results <- vector("list", length(wetlands)) for (i in 1:length(wetlands)) { # print(wetlands[i]) results[[i]] <- get_climate_predictions( wetland = wetlands[i], model = overall_abund ) } combined_df_overall <- purrr::map(results, "summary") %>% bind_rows() ``` ::: Plots like this are in the report, but you can make your own visualisation if you want. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot2::ggplot() + ggplot2::geom_pointrange( data = combined_df_overall |> filter(scenario != "Baseline") |> mutate(scenario = factor(scenario, levels = c("S1", "S4", "S2", "S5", "S3", "S6")), wetland_name = factor(wetland_name, levels = rev(assets_coords))), ggplot2::aes( x = percent_change, xmin = percent_change.lower, xmax = percent_change.upper, y = wetland_name ), # shape = 17, # size = 7 ) + facet_wrap(~scenario, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x",labeller = ggplot2::label_wrap_gen(width = 18)) + ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") + ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 22.5, color = "black", size = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.8) + ggplot2::scale_y_discrete( labels = str_to_title ) + my_theme() + ggplot2::labs( x = "Percent change", y = "Asset" ) + ggplot2::theme( axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), panel.spacing = ggplot2::unit(2, "lines"), panel.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "#F2F2F2"), plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(size = 14), axis.text.y = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 24), legend.position = "none" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](climate_files/figure-html/plot_all_wetlands-1.png){width=1920} ::: :::
